name: India
description: India offers rapid GDP growth, highly dynamic startup ecosystems, and extremely low cost of living, making it an excellent hub for entrepreneurs and local financial experts.

name: Canada
workPermit: PGWP (Post-Graduation Work Permit) up to 3 years
description: Canada is extremely popular for international students due to its clear post-study work pathways, high quality of life, and welcoming immigration laws (PR Express Entry system).

name: Germany
prPossibility: High (EU Blue Card fast track in 21-33 months)
description: Germany features zero tuition fees at public universities, high worker protections, and strong industrial output, but requires learning German for high-level business functions.

name: Australia
description: Australia offers high wages, excellent weather, and a solid economy. However, getting PR has become increasingly competitive under recent point reforms.

name: United Kingdom
workPermit: Graduate Route Visa (2 years work permit)
prPossibility: Medium-Low (Requires skilled worker visa sponsorship for 5 years)
healthcare: National Health Service (NHS) free at point of delivery
description: The UK offers premium global connections, particularly in London's financial district. The 2-year Graduate Route allows students to seek work without sponsorship initially.

name: Singapore
workPermit: Employment Pass (EP) or S-Pass based on salary thresholds
healthcare: World-class private and public co-payment systems
description: Singapore is Asia's financial powerhouse. It offers incredibly low tax rates, safety, and modern infrastructure, but has a high cost of living and strict visa quotas.

name: United States
workPermit: OPT (Optional Practical Training) 1 year, STEM OPT +2 years, H-1B Lottery
prPossibility: Low (Green Card backlog for Indians is extremely long)
description: The USA offers the highest wages and largest corporate markets in the world. However, the visa lottery system (H-1B) and green card delays pose major hurdles.
